blurb:
Princeton University is a private institution that was founded in 1746. In the 2026 edition of Best Colleges, Princeton University is ranked No. #1 in National Universities. It's also ranked No. #1 in Best Value Schools.

It has a total undergraduate enrollment of 5,813 (fall 2024), its setting is suburban, and the campus size is 3,500 acres. The student-faculty ratio at Princeton University is 5:1, and it utilizes a semester-based academic calendar.

Princeton University accepts the Common Application and has a test-optional admissions policy. The school's tuition and fees are $65,210. Sixty-seven percent of first-year students receive need-based financial aid, and the average net price for federal loan recipients is $8,143.

The four-year graduation rate is 75%. Six years after graduation, the median salary for graduates is $87,815.

Massachusetts Institute of Technology does not accept the Common Application. The school's tuition and fees are $64,730. Fifty-six percent of first-year students receive need-based financial aid, and the average net price for federal loan recipients is $20,338.

The four-year graduation rate is 83%. Six years after graduation, the median salary for graduates is $131,633.

institution.footnote: Duke University is no longer admitting new students to its Bachelor of Science in Nursing program, which is expected to graduate its last cohort in spring 2026. They are transitioning their direct-entry, pre-licensure nursing program to a Master of Nursing Degree.
